title: July 5, 2025
excerpt: On emptiness, anxiety, and living on bought time in a foreign country — and why good novels are the only remedy.
excerpt_zh: 关于空虚、焦虑，以及在异国他乡用“买来的时间”生活，还有为什么优秀的小说是唯一的慰藉。

title: Leaving Shanghai
excerpt: A homecoming delayed by two years: a midnight McDonald's, blue self-driving lights on a rebuilt road, and a city loved only through other people's words.
excerpt_zh: 一次推迟两年的返航：午夜的麦当劳、重建道路上幽蓝的自动驾驶灯，以及一座只能通过他人文字去爱的城市。

title: Revisiting the Border City
excerpt: A dusk hike up Victoria Peak that goes wrong, and unknowingly retraces Eileen Chang's daily walk to the University of Hong Kong.
excerpt_zh: 一次入夜后走岔的太平山徒步，无意间重合了张爱玲当年在港大的上学路线。

title: On the Last Day: Farewell, Colleagues
excerpt: 872 days at Specialized. A farewell letter that reads work as a group ride — and choosing, once again, the road less traveled.
excerpt_zh: 在 Specialized 的第 872 天。一封把工作比作团队骑行的告别信，以及再一次选择人迹罕至的那条路。

title: Fool's Day
excerpt: A short story: a grandfather, a bicycle, and a child, in a near future where a virus has wiped the internet from the face of the earth.
excerpt_zh: 一篇小说：在互联网被病毒从地球上抹去的近未来，一位祖父、一辆自行车和一个孩子。

title: Reflection I: GPT
excerpt: From Siri in seventh grade to ChatGPT: I'm not afraid of a future ruled by AI — I'm afraid we're too short-sighted to reach it.
excerpt_zh: 从初一时的 Siri 到 ChatGPT：我不担心被 AI 宰制的未来，我担心我们太短视，活不到那天。

title: Benjamin on Kafka
excerpt: "Plenty of hope — only not for us." On Kafka's refuge, and to whom hope belongs.
excerpt_zh: “有充分的希望——只不过不属于我们罢了。”论卡夫卡的避难所，以及希望究竟属于谁。

title: Wind
excerpt: Shanghai's spring wind is not physical damage but magic damage — a feline creature that toys with its prey.
excerpt_zh: 上海的春风不是物理伤害，而是魔法攻击——一只把玩猎物的猫科动物。

title: Osmanthus Steam
excerpt: Three readings of an Eileen Chang story before finally understanding a single phrase: the humid, fragrant days of early autumn.
excerpt_zh: 读了三遍张爱玲的小说，才终于读懂一个词：初秋潮湿而芬芳的那几天。

title: Summer
excerpt: A lawn, a shade tree, cicadas at eighty decibels, and "Summertime Sadness" on a speaker — a doorway back to childhood.
excerpt_zh: 一片草坪、一棵遮阴的树、80 分贝的蝉鸣，和扬声器里的 Summertime Sadness——一扇通往童年的门。

title: Land
excerpt: During the Shanghai lockdown: on the Chinese bond to the land, the machine of farming civilization, and daring to imagine alternatives.
excerpt_zh: 上海封城期间：论中国人与土地的羁绊、农耕文明的生产机器，以及敢于想象替代方案。

title: A Moment in May
excerpt: A street garden of roses on West Nanjing Road, and the shapes of two strangers crouched to take a photo.
excerpt_zh: 南京西路一座开满蔷薇的街心花园，和一对蹲着拍照的陌生人的身影。

title: World Book Day, 2022
excerpt: Rereading Eileen Chang's essays during lockdown, and finding new recognition in words once dismissed as fussy and sentimental.
excerpt_zh: 封城期间重读张爱玲的散文，在曾被认为“婆婆妈妈”的文字里找到崭新的认同。

title: Dick in a Box
excerpt: A birthday confession, ten months and two weeks later. A moth that has circled long, at last plunging into the flame.
excerpt_zh: 十个月零两周之后的一封生日告白。一只盘旋已久的飞蛾，终于冲进那团火。

title: On Nietzsche's Genealogy of Morals
excerpt: Master morality and slave morality, the inversion wrought by "love," and the question Nietzsche points to but never answers.
excerpt_zh: 主人道德与奴隶道德、由“爱”实现的颠倒，以及尼采指出却从未回答的问题。

title: The Boundary of the Real in the Brain-in-a-Vat Problem
title_zh: “钵中之脑”问题中真实的边界
excerpt: Can a brain in a vat know it is one? On Putnam, reference, and the one thing that is real: the world is not false the way we imagine.
excerpt_zh: 钵中之脑能否意识到自己是钵中之脑？论普特南、指称，以及唯一真实的事：世界不以我们设想的方式虚假。

title: The Poetic Turn of Mimesis
excerpt: How Aristotle freed art from technique and Plato's censure, turning "mimesis" from metaphysics toward an independent aesthetics.
excerpt_zh: 亚里士多德如何将艺术从技术与柏拉图的非难中解放，令“摹仿说”从形而上学转向独立的美学。
